title: Doc adoption: the wiki takes over stale docs — healed, wiki-managed copies; originals preserved and auto-released
context: Owner directive: once a doc grades do-not-trust, the wiki must take it in — copy it, heal it, and manage it from there — so the wiki alone is a sufficient reading surface. The prior ledger only graded and routed; its rejection of 'copying doc prose' targeted a second EDITABLE truth, which adoption avoids by making the copy a generated artifact.
decision: Every STALE doc is automatically ADOPTED during hunch wiki / --heal (src/wiki/adopt.ts): a wiki-managed copy lands at <wiki>/docs/<rel-kebab>.md, healed against the graph deterministically — each pin to a superseded/missing decision is RE-PINNED to the current one with a '🧭 Graph correction' callout injected after it (current decision text + rejected alternatives), non-pin issues surface in a top banner, and the full original prose is preserved inside the copy. The ORIGINAL file is never touched ('never rewrite prose' applies to user files; the copy is a hunch:wiki artifact, excluded from doc scanning). Manifest entries use the reserved 'doc:<rel>' component id; the freshness hash covers INPUTS (source content + current decision per pinned topic + issues), so a source edit or graph move re-heals the copy via the existing wiki-stale drift. Lifecycle is symmetric: when the original becomes grounded again (human healed + re-pinned it) or the source is deleted, the copy retires automatically on the next heal. The ledger and component pages route stale docs to their adopted copies — the wiki is now the complete reading surface.
consequences: Adopted copies are committed under wiki/docs/ in the public home (sources are public repo docs — no leak) and live in the overlay repo for --private (union-healed corrections may quote private decisions there only)
A stale doc now yields TWO ledger artifacts: the grade (specs.md) and the copy — both freshness-gated, healed by the same hunch wiki --heal
Copies with no topic pins (e.g. stale only via dead code refs) adopt with banner-only healing — honest: the wiki flags what it cannot correct
The 'doc:' manifest namespace joins '_specs' as reserved ids excluded from component-orphan logic
alternatives_rejected: Rewriting the ORIGINAL doc in place: crosses the never-rewrite-user-prose line; adoption heals a generated copy instead
LLM-rewritten adopted copies: nondeterministic healing; re-pin + correction callouts convey the current truth deterministically (LLM prose could layer on later behind the existing draftProse pattern)
Keeping the copy after the original heals: two live versions of a healthy doc; retirement on re-grounding keeps exactly one readable truth per doc
Marker banners injected into originals to point at the copy: still a write into user prose; routing lives in the wiki (ledger + component pages) instead
